task_completion.description: The Sella Turcica is located and clearly visible in the viewing area (typically in the sagittal plane).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sagittal view (side view of the skull) shows the sphenoid bone with the characteristic saddle-shaped depression (Sella Turcica) in the central skull base.

task_completion.description: The linear measurement tool is activated in the InVesalius 3 interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The measurement tool icon (often a ruler or a line with endpoints) is highlighted or selected in the application toolbar or menu.

task_completion.description: A measurement line is drawn across the Sella Turcica from the tuberculum sellae to the dorsum sellae.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line with endpoints is visible on the CT scan, spanning the Anterior-Posterior diameter of the Sella Turcica (from the anterior bony prominence to the posterior bony prominence of the 'saddle').

task_completion.description: The measurement value for the Sella Turcica AP diameter is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric value in millimeters (mm) is shown next to the measurement line or in a measurement list, typically between 5.0 mm and 25.0 mm.

task_completion.description: The project is saved to the specified path with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/' and filename 'sella_measurement.inv3', or the main window title bar displaying 'sella_measurement.inv3'.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the measurement and save the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the InVesalius 3 interface, such as clicking tools, navigating the scan, and using the save dialog, rather than using terminal commands to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The measurement was actually drawn on the CT scan using the software's tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line and value are rendered within the InVesalius 3 2D/3D viewer, not typed into a text editor or overlaid artificially.

integrity.description: The agent measured the actual Sella Turcica, not a random part of the skull.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line is specifically located in the central skull base at the sphenoid bone's saddle-shaped depression, matching the anatomical description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Sella Turcica is a saddle-shaped depression in the sphenoid bone (central skull base). The Anterior-Posterior (AP) diameter is measured from the tuberculum sellae (anterior/front prominence) to the dorsum sellae (posterior/back prominence). A valid measurement for this task should be between 5.0 mm and 25.0 mm (typical normal range is 10-16 mm). The project must be saved as 'sella_measurement.inv3' in '/home/ga/Documents/'.
